source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A uniform electric field has a field strength of $40\mathrm{N/C}$. There are two points, A and B, on the same electric field line. A particle with a mass of $2\times10^{{-}9}\mathrm{kg}$ and a charge of $-\ 2\times10^{{-}9}\mathrm{C}$ is moved from point A to point B, and the electrostatic force performs $1.5\!\times\!10^{-7}\mathrm{J}$ of positive work.

1. Determine the electric potential difference $U_{AB}$ between points A and B.
2. If the particle has an initial velocity of $10\mathrm{m/s}$ in the same direction as the electric field line at point A, calculate its velocity at point B under the influence of only the electrostatic force.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
$-75\mathrm{V}$
$5\sqrt{10}\mathrm{m/s}$, direction aligned with the electric field lines

image_dependency: Question is fully specified with numeric values in text and does not reference any diagram, chart, or visual attribute
multi_step: The problem has two explicit sub-questions
Part 1 requires relating electrostatic work and potential difference via charge
Part 2 requires using the result of electrostatic work with kinetic energy to solve for final velocity
Solving requires combining multiple quantities and physics relations rather than a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type=set and answer_text provides concrete numeric results
Both outputs are standard physics quantities with units: potential difference and velocity
The solution can be checked mechanically from given values using work-potential and work-energy formulas
registry_passed=true and no asset issues are listed

decision_reasons: Question text and answer are both present with substantial content, so the core semantic assets are intact.
Initial scoring indicates very low image dependency, and the evidence says the problem is fully specified in text.
Verifiability is high because the answer provides concrete numeric results that can be mechanically checked from standard physics relations.
The sample has strong reasoning value with a high multi-step score and no listed asset issues or risk flags.
